summary: Quantum physics explains how matter, energy, and information behave at microscopic scales where probability, superposition, and quantization dominate. Modern coverage of the field usually connects foundational concepts such as wave functions and uncertainty with practical applications such as quantum computing, sensing, secure communication, and advanced materials. Across academic, government, and scientific reference sources, the recurring pattern is that quantum theory is mathematically precise, experimentally verified, and still central to active research on measurement, entanglement, and scalable technology.
aiOverview: Quantum physics studies microscopic systems using quantized states, probabilistic measurement, and wave-like behavior.
Current reporting often links foundational theory to quantum computing, sensing, materials science, and secure communication.

results.title: Quantum mechanics | Definition, Development, and Equations
results.snippet: Quantum mechanics describes physical systems with discrete states, wave functions, and probabilities rather than classical certainty. Reference overviews emphasize superposition, measurement, and the historical shift from classical mechanics to a probabilistic framework that still predicts experiments with high precision.

results.title: Quantum Information Science
results.snippet: Department of Energy material presents quantum information science as the bridge between theory and technology, covering computing, networking, and sensing. It highlights how superposition and entanglement create capabilities that are difficult to reproduce with classical devices.

results.title: Quantum Physics and Quantum Information
results.snippet: University course material introduces the mathematical structure behind wave functions, operators, eigenvalues, and time evolution. It also shows how textbook quantum ideas connect to problem solving, interference, and the measurement postulate.

results.title: Quantum entanglement
results.snippet: Philosophical and technical reference material describes entanglement as a nonclassical correlation that prevents composite systems from being reduced to independent parts. It is central to debates about locality, realism, and how quantum states represent shared information.

results.title: Quantum computing and quantum simulation
results.snippet: Research coverage connects quantum physics to computing architectures, simulation, and the difficulty of preserving coherent states in hardware. It emphasizes error rates, scaling, and the experimental reality that useful systems require both theoretical and engineering advances.

results.title: Quantum sensing
results.snippet: Measurement agencies describe quantum sensing as the use of coherence, interference, and entanglement to improve timing, navigation, imaging, and field detection. The field shows how abstract quantum principles can translate into practical precision advantages.

results.title: Quantum physics
results.snippet: CERN education material frames quantum physics as the theory of particles, fields, and probabilities underlying high energy experiments. It highlights uncertainty, wave particle duality, and how the theory supports the Standard Model while still raising open questions.

results.title: Quantum mechanics
results.snippet: Physics reporting surveys the major principles of quantum mechanics and explains why the theory remains indispensable for electronics, chemistry, and information science. Contemporary articles often connect classroom ideas to the broader innovation cycle around quantum technologies.
